Figure 2: The overall architecture of MASS-RAG, illustrating multi-agent filtering and answer synthesis when the Answer Agent is enabled.
Figure 9: MASS-RAG Case Study 5 - Dataset: TriviaQA, Model: Llama38B

Figure 4: Performance comparison of MASS-RAG with state-of-the-art baselines across four benchmark datasets. The left panel reports results compared with Self-RAG using Llama27B, while the right panel shows comparisons with MAIN-RAG based on Llama38B. In both cases, the backbone models are kept consistent to ensure a fair comparison.
Figure 3: Evidence Coverage Rate (ECR) of each filter response on the Uniquely Attributable Subset of TriviaQA and PopQA using Llama38B and Mistral7B with the top-10 retrieved documents.
Figure 10: MASS-RAG Case Study 6 - Dataset: TriviaQA, Model: Llama38B
Figure 8: MASS-RAG Case Study 4 - Dataset: TriviaQA, Model: Llama38B

Table 1: Overall experimental results on four tasks. Bold numbers refer to the best performance among baselines without retrieval and training-free baselines, and underline numbers refer to the second-best performance. ∗ indicates concurrent results conducted by recent works or original papers. For the metrics, acc, em, rg, and mau denote accuracy, str-em, rouge, and MAUVE, respectively.
Figure 7: MASS-RAG Case Study 3 - Dataset: PopQA, Model: Mistral7B
